headline: Big Oil Has Transformed Into Shareholder-Friendly Cash Machines, Says CNBC’s Halftime Report

headline: ExxonMobil Crushes Q1 2026 EPS Estimates by 15% — Here’s What Investors Need to Know

headline: S&P 500 Faces Oil-Fueled Sell-off Pressure in Possible Return to Merger Monday Deal
summary: The S&P 500 (^GSPC) opened the week lower as oil prices spiked on renewed Iran tensions, with index futures slipping 0.1% after conflicting reports about a U.S. warship near the Strait of Hormuz. WTI crude futures jumped 3% to trade above $105 per barrel, and Brent climbed 3% to above $111, pulling capital into energy names. Futures recovered once ... headline: XOM Q1 Deep Dive: Operational Flexibility and Portfolio Diversity Amid Global Energy Disruptions
summary: Integrated energy company ExxonMobil (NYSE:XOM) reported Q1 CY2026 results beating Wall Street’s revenue expectations, with sales up 2.4% year on year to $85.14 billion. Its non-GAAP profit of $1.16 per share was 15.1% above analysts’ consensus estimates.

headline: Exxon Mobil Weighs Venezuela Return As Valuation Signals Potential Upside
summary: Exxon Mobil (NYSE:XOM) is assessing potential investments in Venezuela following recent regulatory changes that reopen the country’s oil sector to global companies. The company is reviewing options to re enter a market that previously saw widespread nationalization of foreign owned assets. Any decision could affect Exxon Mobil’s global portfolio exposure to one of the world’s largest oil reserves. For a company of Exxon Mobil’s size, new access to a resource heavy country like Venezuela...
